name: Media Members Federal Credit Union
description_short: Penn Credit Union is a Philadelphia-based credit union with two branches offering checking, savings, credit cards, and online banking via their Virtual Branch platform.
description_long:
Penn Credit Union (PennCU) is a member-owned credit union headquartered at 3131 Walnut Street in Philadelphia, PA 19104. The institution operates at least two physical locations — the main Walnut Street branch and a newer Spruce Street branch — serving the greater Philadelphia community. As a credit union, it operates on a not-for-profit cooperative model, returning value to members rather than outside shareholders.

PennCU offers a range of core banking services including checking and savings accounts, two credit card programs (DXOnline cards beginning with 4169 and EZ Cards beginning with 4052), and debit card access. Members can manage accounts through the Virtual Branch online portal, which supports balance checks, transaction history, and fund transfers. The credit union also partners with Sallie Mae for student loan products and TruStage for member insurance, and offers Mechanical Repair Coverage as an add-on product.

What distinguishes PennCU is its participation in two major credit union networks: CU$ (a non-surcharging ATM network providing fee-free cash access) and Co-Op Shared Branching, which allows members to conduct transactions at thousands of participating credit union branches nationwide. Sprig Mobile Banking rounds out the digital access options for on-the-go account management.

The main limitation of evaluating PennCU is that their website — particularly the Virtual Branch page — discloses very little about rates, fees, loan products, or membership eligibility criteria. Prospective members should contact the credit union directly at 267-969-7011 to determine if they qualify and to compare rates before opening an account. For existing members, the multi-channel access (two branches, online, mobile, shared branching, ATM network) represents solid day-to-day convenience. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Access to CU$ non-surcharging ATM network — no ATM fees at participating machines
Co-Op Shared Branching participation enables in-person transactions at thousands of credit unions nationwide
Two Philadelphia branch locations including a newly opened Spruce Street branch
Virtual Branch online banking supports balance checks, history viewing, and fund transfers
Sprig Mobile Banking app available for mobile account access
Two separate credit card programs with dedicated lost/stolen card hotlines
Sallie Mae student loan partnership provides access to student financing
cons: Website discloses no information on membership eligibility requirements
No rates, APR ranges, fees, or account terms published on the reviewed page
Physical branch presence limited to Philadelphia — limited geographic reach
Sparse online presence makes independent product comparison difficult before joining
best_for: Philadelphia residents seeking a local not-for-profit credit union alternative to big banks
Students or families needing access to Sallie Mae student loans through a credit union
Members who travel and want nationwide branch access via Co-Op Shared Branching
Consumers who want fee-free ATM access through the CU$ network
diagnosis: Penn Credit Union is best for Philadelphia-area residents who want a community credit union with solid digital banking tools and the added benefit of nationwide branch and ATM access through cooperative networks. The primary caveat is that the website provides almost no transparency on rates, fees, or membership eligibility — prospective members must call or visit a branch to get the details needed to make an informed decision.
services: Virtual Branch online banking (balances, history, transfers)
Credit cards — DXOnline program (cards starting with 4169)
Credit cards — EZ Card program (cards starting with 4052)
Co-Op Shared Branching (nationwide credit union branch access)
meta_description: Penn Credit Union is a Philadelphia-based credit union offering checking, savings, credit cards, and online banking.
